mapping.a_element: large ensemble of interacting economic agents
mapping.b_element: population of coupled stochastic KaiC circadian oscillators

mapping.a_element: emergence of a collective macroscopic state (crashes, excess volatility, collective rhythms of boom/bust)
mapping.b_element: emergence of a collective synchronized rhythm

mapping.a_element: self-organized-criticality / marginally-stable state reached by the system

brief_justification: Paper B's machinery is a specific coherence-driven synchronization phase transition of coupled stochastic oscillators (a Kuramoto-type transition whose single control parameter is single-oscillator coherence), whereas Paper A is a broad phenomenological review whose three recurring mechanisms are multiplicity of history-dependent equilibria, failure to reach equilibrium giving chaos, and self-organized criticality — none of which is a coupled-oscillator synchronization process. The only overlap is the generic notion of a phase transition to a collective emergent state in an interacting many-unit system, with no shared governing equation or role-for-role control parameter, so they do not share underlying machinery.
